title: achyranthes pilosa
intro: Achyranthes pilosa, also known as white snake root or white gromwell, is a plant species in the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Achyranthes pilosa include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections, such as respiratory and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
